title: Spring with a plurality of elements, and actuator including such a spring
abstract: A spring (3, 3') comprising a plurality of elements (30), each element (3) comprising a rigid portion (31) and a flexible beam (32), the extremities (320, 321) of the flexible beam being supported by the rigid portion (31), the flexible beam (32) having a single stable position, so that the flexible beam can be deformed when a pressure is exerted between said extremities in the direction of the rigid portion (31), and returns to said single stable position when the pressure is released, and wherein the rigid portion (31) of at least one element (30) is in contact with the flexible beam (32) of the next element between said extremities (320, 321) of the flexible beam (32), so that the spring has a negative stiffness over an operating range.The arrangement ensures a pure radial compression/expansion of the spring.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A spring (3, 3') comprising a plurality of elements (30), each element (3) comprising a rigid portion (31) and a flexible beam (32), the extremities (320, 321) of the flexible beam being supported by the rigid portion (31), the flexible beam (32) having a single stable position, so that the flexible beam can be deformed when a pressure is exerted between said extremities in the direction of the rigid portion (31), and returns to said single stable position when the pressure is released, and wherein the rigid portion (31) of at least one element (30) is in contact with the flexible beam (32) of the next element between said extremities (320, 321) of the flexible beam (32), so that the spring has a negative stiffness over an operating range 2. Spring according to claim 1, said flexible beam (32) being curved between its two extremities (320, 321). 3. Spring according to claim 2, said flexible beam (32) being curved in two planes (a, b). 4. Spring according to any of the claims 1 to 3, said rigid portion (31) comprising a prominent extension (310) connected to the center of said flexible beam (32) of the next element (30). 5. Spring according to any of the claims 1 to 4, formed as a cylinder with N said elements (30), the flexible beam (32) of the first element (30) being in contact with the rigid portion (31) of the Nth element, so that the diameter of said cylinder is reduced when each rigid portion (31) exerts a pressure on the flexible beam (32) of the next element (30). 6. Spring (3') according to any of the claims 1 to 5, comprising a plurality of rows of said elements (30A, 30B), so that each the rigid portion 

 (31') of each element (30A) in one row is integrally connected with the rigid portion (31') of another element (30B) in an adjacent row. 7. Spring (3') according to claim 6, comprising an even number of rows. 8. Spring (3') according to any of the claims 6 to 7, adjacent elements (30A, 30B) being oriented head-to-tail, so that the flexible beams (32) of the elements in one row (30A) are connected to the rigid support (31') of the next element (30A), while the flexible beams (32) of the elements (30B) in another row are connected to the rigid supports (31') of the previous element (30B). 9. Spring according to any of the claims 1 to 8, arranged to ensure a pure radial compression/expansion. 10. Actuator comprising a layer of electroactive polymer (1) and a spring (3, 3') according to any of the claims 1 to 9 for pre-stretching the electroactive polymer. 11. An implantable ventricular assist device comprising an actuator according to claim 10. 12. A sphincter comprising an actuator according to claim 10.
